name: Security Review
description: Scans source files for common security anti-patterns and dangerous API usage

checks.fail: ❌ No package.json — cannot perform dependency analysis

checks.pass: ✅ No eval() usage found in src/
checks.fail: 🔴 eval() detected — arbitrary code execution risk
failRecommendations: Replace eval() with safer alternatives (JSON.parse, Function constructors are also dangerous)
Audit all dynamic code execution paths
Sanitize all HTML content before rendering
Use DOMPurify or a similar library
Replace with safe text interpolation wherever possible

checks.pass: ✅ No hardcoded credentials detected in src/
failRecommendations: Move all secrets to environment variables or a secrets manager
Rotate any secrets that were committed
Add a pre-commit hook (e.g. gitleaks) to prevent future leaks

checks.pass: ✅ No direct child_process usage in src/ (or acceptable usage found)
checks.fail: ⚠️  child_process imported in source files — review for command injection
failRecommendations: Sanitize all user-supplied data before passing to exec/spawn
Prefer execFile over exec to avoid shell injection

checks.prompt: Review the package.json dependencies and devDependencies for packages with known security vulnerabilities, deprecated packages, or packages commonly associated with supply-chain attacks. Flag any findings as CRITICAL if they have active CVEs or are known malicious packages.

checks.pass: ✅ No obvious command injection patterns (exec + user input) detected
checks.fail: 🔴 Possible command injection: exec() called with apparent user-controlled input
failRecommendations: Never pass user input directly to exec/execSync
Use execFile with an argument array, or a purpose-built shell-quoting library
Validate and allowlist all user-supplied command arguments

checks.command: grep -rE "SQL|query|knex|prisma" src --include="*.ts" --include="*.js" -l 2>/dev/null | xargs grep -lE "\$\{|` [^`]*\+" 2>/dev/null | head -10 || true
checks.pass: ✅ No obvious SQL string-interpolation patterns detected
checks.fail: 🔴 Possible SQL injection: string interpolation near database query keywords
failRecommendations: Use parameterised queries or an ORM (never string-interpolate user input)
Audit all query() calls for unescaped template literals

checks.pass: ✅ CORS configuration found — verify origin is not wildcard in production
checks.fail: ℹ️  No explicit CORS configuration detected
failRecommendations: Ensure CORS origin is restricted to known domains in production

checks.fail: ℹ️  No JWT signing calls found in src/

checks.prompt:
Perform a deep security review of the TypeScript source in src/.

OWASP Top-10 focus areas:
1. A01 Broken Access Control — missing auth middleware, horizontal privilege escalation
2. A02 Cryptographic Failures — weak algorithms, missing TLS enforcement, plaintext secrets
3. A03 Injection — SQL, command, path traversal, SSTI
4. A05 Security Misconfiguration — debug flags left on, overly permissive CORS
5. A07 Identification & Authentication — JWT algorithm confusion, missing expiry
6. A10 SSRF — user-supplied URLs passed to fetch/http without validation

Directory listing:
{content}

Prefix critical exploitable issues with CRITICAL:, serious concerns with WARN:, best-practice notes with INFO:.
checks.fail: 🔴 Security issues identified in LLM review

checks.body:
## 🔐 Security Review

The automated security review has completed. Check the run findings for details.
checks.pass: ✅ GitHub comment posted with security review results
checks.fail: ⚠️  Could not post GitHub comment (GITHUB_TOKEN / PR number not set)
